rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Salt, Decoded — What Salt Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: High fructose corn syrup (HFCS)
rows.what: Industrial sweetener — higher fructose than sucrose
rows.kip: Observational studies link to metabolic syndrome, fatty liver, and obesity. More metabolically disruptive than sucrose at equivalent doses.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Glucose-fructose syrup
rows.what: EU name for HFCS — same product, different label
rows.kip: Same as HFCS. Worth flagging. Common in soft drinks and sauces.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Glucose syrup
rows.kip: High GI. Rapidly absorbed. Common in confectionery and baked goods.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Corn syrup
rows.kip: High GI. Rapidly absorbed as glucose.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Invert sugar syrup
rows.what: Sucrose split into glucose and fructose — prevents crystallisation
rows.kip: Higher fructose content than sucrose. Used in confectionery and jams.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Golden syrup
rows.kip: Still sugar. High GI. Natural-sounding but no nutritional advantage over sucrose.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Maple syrup
rows.kip: Natural but still sugar. Slightly lower GI than glucose syrup. Trace minerals present.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Barley malt syrup
rows.kip: High GI. Maltose-heavy. Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Sucrose
rows.kip: The reference point. All other sugars are compared to this.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Fructose
rows.what: Fruit sugar — sweeter than sucrose, metabolised by liver
rows.kip: At high doses, fructose is metabolised differently to glucose — linked to fatty liver and metabolic syndrome. Fine in whole fruit context.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Lactose
rows.kip: Natural in dairy. Low GI. Relevant to lactose intolerance.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Galactose
rows.kip: Lower GI than glucose. Still a sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Maltose
rows.what: Two glucose molecules — from malted grains
rows.kip: Very high GI — higher than table sugar. Common in malted products.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Raw sugar / raw cane sugar
rows.kip: Trace minerals but nutritionally equivalent to white sugar. Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Cane sugar
rows.kip: Same as table sugar. Natural-sounding, same metabolic effect.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Beet sugar
rows.kip: Chemically identical to cane sugar. Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Coconut sugar
rows.kip: Slightly lower GI than sucrose. Contains inulin. Still primarily sucrose. Often over-marketed as healthy.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Palm sugar
rows.kip: Sucrose-dominant. Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Date sugar
rows.kip: Contains fibre which slows absorption. Still sugar. Lower GI than sucrose.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Molasses
rows.what: By-product of sugar refining — dark and thick
rows.kip: Higher mineral content than refined sugar but still primarily sucrose.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Muscovado sugar
rows.kip: Trace minerals. Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Demerara sugar
rows.kip: Still sugar. No meaningful nutritional advantage.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Turbinado sugar
rows.kip: Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Icing sugar / powdered sugar
rows.kip: Sucrose. Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Maltodextrin
rows.what: Processed starch — rapidly absorbed as sugar
rows.kip: Very high GI. Absorbed faster than sugar despite not tasting sweet. Common in UPF as bulking agent.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Dextrin
rows.kip: High GI. Metabolised as glucose. Common in cereals and snacks.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Modified food starch
rows.kip: Can be rapidly digested depending on modification. UPF marker.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Hydrolysed starch
rows.what: Starch broken down into sugars by enzymes or acid
rows.kip: Mixture of glucose, maltose, and dextrins. High GI.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Corn starch (as sweetener)
rows.what: Starch from maize — base for glucose syrup
rows.kip: High GI when used as a sweetening agent rather than a thickener.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Waxy maize starch
rows.kip: Very high GI. Often used in sports products.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Fruit juice concentrate
rows.what: Fruit with water removed — concentrated fructose
rows.kip: Often used to sweeten products. Despite fruit origin, behaves like added sugar once concentrated.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: White grape juice concentrate
rows.kip: Colourless so often invisible in products. Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Pear juice concentrate
rows.kip: High fructose. Common in baby food sweetening.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Date paste / date syrup
rows.what: Blended dates — whole food but high sugar
rows.kip: Contains fibre which slows absorption. Still high in fructose and glucose.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Dried fruit (as sweetener)
rows.kip: Without water, sugar density is much higher than fresh fruit. Fine as whole food, different when used as a sweetener.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Sucrose
rows.kip: Table sugar. The reference point.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: D-fructose
rows.kip: Same metabolic concerns as fructose — liver metabolism, fatty liver link at high doses.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: D-glucose
rows.kip: Blood sugar. Rapidly absorbed. Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Saccharose
rows.what: Alternative name for sucrose — used in some EU labelling
rows.kip: Table sugar. Still sugar.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Anhydrous dextrose
rows.what: Glucose with water removed — used in food manufacturing
rows.kip: Very high GI. Used in confectionery and sports products.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Crystalline fructose
rows.kip: 100% fructose. Same liver metabolism concerns as HFCS. Worth flagging.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Ethyl maltol
rows.what: Flavour enhancer that makes food taste sweeter
rows.kip: Not a sugar but makes food taste sweeter — can mask sugar content perception.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Caramel (as sweetener)
rows.what: Heated sugar — adds colour and sweetness
rows.kip: Still sugar. Worth noting when it appears high on ingredients list.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Trehalose
rows.kip: Naturally occurring but used industrially. Some research links to gut bacteria changes.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Isoglucose
rows.kip: Same as HFCS. High fructose. Worth flagging.
rows.page_title: Hidden Names for Sugar, Decoded — What Sugar Really Looks Like on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Sucralose (E955)
rows.what: ~600× sweetness vs sucrose. EFSA ADI 15 mg/kg/day
rows.kip: Heat-stable, widely used in baked goods, soft drinks, and tabletop sweeteners (Splenda).
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Saccharin (E954)
rows.what: ~300× sweetness vs sucrose. EFSA ADI 5 mg/kg/day
rows.kip: The oldest artificial sweetener (1879). Tabletop and beverage use. Less common in modern UK formulations as ace-K and sucralose have largely replaced it.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Neotame (E961)
rows.what: ~7,000–13,000× sweetness vs sucrose. EFSA ADI 2 mg/kg/day
rows.kip: Aspartame derivative without the PKU warning (the chemistry blocks phenylalanine release). Very high sweetness multiple means tiny quantities are used.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Advantame (E969)
rows.what: ~20,000× sweetness vs sucrose. EFSA ADI 5 mg/kg/day
rows.kip: Newest aspartame derivative (approved EU 2014). Heat-stable. Used at extremely low concentrations.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Cyclamate (E952)
rows.what: ~30–50× sweetness vs sucrose. EFSA ADI 7 mg/kg/day
rows.kip: Approved in UK and EU. Banned in the USA since 1969 (the FDA reviewed but never reversed; petitions remain pending). Often combined with saccharin in tabletop sweeteners.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Salt of aspartame-acesulfame (E962)
rows.what: ~350× sweetness vs sucrose. ADIs of components apply
rows.kip: A pre-blended salt of the two sweeteners. Counts toward both individual ADIs.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Steviol glycosides from fermentation (E960c)
rows.what: Steviol glycosides produced by fermentation rather than plant extraction
rows.kip: EU-approved 2021 as an alternative production route to plant extraction. Same chemical structure as plant-derived.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Thaumatin (E957)
rows.what: ~2,000× sweetness vs sucrose. EFSA: no numerical ADI — safe at intended use levels
rows.kip: Protein-based sweetener from the African katemfe fruit. Mostly used as a flavour modifier rather than primary sweetener.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Tagatose (E913 in some lists)
rows.what: Rare sugar with ~92% sweetness of sucrose
rows.kip: Naturally occurring sugar. Approved as a novel food in EU. Lower glycaemic response than sucrose but still a carbohydrate.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Mannitol (E421)
rows.kip: Lower laxative threshold than sorbitol. Common in sugar-free hard candies. Same >10% warning rule.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Xylitol (E967)
rows.what: ~100% sweetness of sucrose (same as sugar). Laxative threshold ~40g
rows.kip: Most-similar polyol to sucrose in sweetness. Dental benefits (reduces oral bacterial growth). Common in sugar-free gum, mints, dental products. Highly toxic to dogs — even small amounts can cause hypoglycaemia and liver failure. Mandatory polyol warning at >10%.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Lactitol (E966)
rows.kip: Less common than other polyols. Used in some confectionery and bakery. Mandatory polyol warning at >10%.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Isomalt (E953)
rows.kip: Common in sugar-free hard candies and decorative sugar work. Mandatory polyol warning at >10%.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Polyglycitol syrup (E964)
rows.kip: Mixture of polyols including maltitol, sorbitol, and hydrogenated glucose syrups. Common in sugar-free confectionery. Mandatory polyol warning at >10%.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Sugar + sweetener combinations
rows.what: Reformulated products keeping some sugar plus added high-intensity sweeteners
rows.kip: Common after the Soft Drinks Industry Levy — manufacturers reduce sugar to below the 5g/100ml lower-band threshold while adding sweeteners to maintain perceived sweetness. The label shows reduced sugar but the sweetness is unchanged.
rows.page_title: Sweeteners (Non-Sugar), Decoded — Hidden Names on a Label · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: RSPCA Assured
rows.what: Welfare-focused certification — higher than legal minimum, species-specific
rows.kip: Independently audited welfare standard. Stricter than Red Tractor on welfare specifically. Common on chicken, pork, eggs, salmon.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Soil Association Organic
rows.what: UK organic certifier — standards above EU baseline
rows.kip: Most common UK organic mark. Stricter standards than the EU Organic logo on some categories. Covers food safety, animal welfare, and environmental practice.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: LEAF Marque
rows.what: Linking Environment And Farming — integrated farm management standards
rows.kip: Sustainability-focused; not the same as organic. Common on UK fresh produce.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: British Lion Mark (eggs)
rows.what: British Egg Industry Council mark — salmonella-vaccinated hens, traceability, Code of Practice
rows.kip: On nearly all UK retail eggs. Confirms vaccination, on-farm hygiene, and the printed egg-code traceability. NHS guidance is that Lion-marked eggs are safe to eat raw or partly cooked, including for pregnant women, the very young, and the elderly.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Rainforest Alliance / UTZ
rows.kip: Merged in 2018; the green-frog mark covers the combined programme. Common on coffee, cocoa, tea, bananas.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: B Corp
rows.what: Certification of overall social and environmental performance (whole company, not just product)
rows.kip: Some food brands carry B Corp certification on packs. Whole-company audit; product-level claims still need their own basis.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: MSC (Marine Stewardship Council)
rows.kip: Most common UK sustainable fish mark. Covers fishery practice and chain-of-custody. Specifically for wild-caught fish; farmed fish use ASC instead.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: ASC (Aquaculture Stewardship Council)
rows.kip: The MSC equivalent for farmed fish (salmon, tilapia, prawns, etc.). Covers feed, environmental impact, and worker rights.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Marine Conservation Society Good Fish Guide
rows.what: Consumer guide / rating — not a pack-level mark
rows.kip: Useful for shoppers but rarely printed on packs. Available as a free app/website. Rates fish by species + catch method + region.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: \"Suitable for vegetarians\" / \"Suitable for vegans\" (text only)
rows.kip: These claims are allowed without certification. Most UK retailers self-declare based on ingredient lists. Audited marks (Vegan Society, Vegetarian Society) add the verification layer.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Halal Monitoring Committee (HMC)
rows.what: UK halal certifier — the strictest of the major UK schemes
rows.kip: HMC is the most-recognised UK halal mark for hand-slaughtered, no-stunning standards. Other UK halal certifiers operate to different standards (some accept pre-stunning; HMC does not).
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Halal Food Authority (HFA)
rows.kip: HFA accepts mechanical pre-stunning; HMC does not. Different schools of thought; both are recognised halal in the UK.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Halal Food & Culinary Excellence (HFCE)
rows.kip: Smaller UK certifier. Standards documented and audited.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Plastic resin code (1–7 inside Möbius)
rows.kip: 1 = PET (bottles, common). 2 = HDPE (milk bottles). 3 = PVC (rare in food). 4 = LDPE (films). 5 = PP (yoghurt pots, common). 6 = PS (polystyrene, mostly retired). 7 = OTHER (mixed). Numbers 1, 2, and 5 are the most-recyclable in UK kerbside; check the OPRL label first regardless.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Compostable (Seedling logo, EN 13432)
rows.what: Industrially compostable — commercial composting facility only
rows.kip: Looks recyclable but goes in food-waste collection (industrial composting), NOT garden compost or kerbside recycling. Requires industrial heat to break down. Composting infrastructure varies by local authority.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Green Dot (Der Grüne Punkt)
rows.what: European packaging recovery scheme — NOT a UK recyclability mark
rows.kip: Common on imported packs but means the manufacturer paid into a packaging-recovery scheme in the source country. Says NOTHING about whether the pack is recyclable in the UK. Ignore for UK recycling decisions.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Oven temperature symbol (°C / Gas Mark)
rows.kip: Always paired with a time. Fan-oven temperatures are typically 20°C lower than conventional. Gas Mark conversions: 1 = 140°C, 4 = 180°C, 7 = 220°C.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Refrigerate (snowflake-free)
rows.what: Keep refrigerated — specific temperature usually given
rows.kip: UK chilled food storage standard is below 8°C; most domestic fridges run 0–5°C. Mandatory pictogram on perishable food. Failure to refrigerate within the specified time can shift use-by safety status.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: \"Once opened, consume within X days\"
rows.kip: Often shorter than the printed use-by date. After opening, the post-opening period takes over.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: Choking hazard / age warning
rows.what: Pictogram or text warning small parts unsuitable for under-3s
rows.kip: Common on small confectionery, toys-in-cereal, certain food-shape products. Mandatory under UK Toy Safety regulations and the FSA guidance on small-piece food.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library

rows.name: PAO (period after opening) symbol
rows.what: Open-jar pictogram with a number — cosmetics-specific, sometimes on edible oils
rows.kip: Common on cosmetics; less so on food but appears on some olive oils, infused oils, and condiments. The number indicates months of stability after opening.
rows.page_title: Symbols & Trustmarks, Decoded — Every Logo and Pictogram on a UK Food Pack · SCANSMART Knowledge Library
